_comment: Connector-specific edits to GENERATED files that a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present and fails CI if a reprint dropped one. status: active = connector-only, a reprint would clobber it; upstreamed = also fixed in the press but the back-port must still be present today.

handfixes.summary: sync injects `select * from <Entity>` (+ minorversion) for the /query endpoint; QBO has no plain REST list path
handfixes.why: Every QuickBooks Online entity is read through /query, which REQUIRES a SQL-like `query` parameter. The generated sync path sent a bare /query with no query text, and QBO answers HTTP 200 with a `{"Fault":...SystemFailureError}` envelope (not a 4xx). The generic extractor finds no array, so upsertSingleObject gets the Fault envelope, extractObjectID returns "", and every resource fails with `missing id for <resource>` (0 rows). Fix injects a per-entity `select * from <Entity> ...` and `minorversion=75` when path=="/query", mapping each sync resource to its PascalCase QBO entity.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Press change: teach cli-printing-press to inject the entity SQL + minorversion for QBO-shaped /query endpoints via a sync-hint / x-pp-query spec extension, so a reprint regenerates this by construction. File via /printing-press-retro.

handfixes.summary: sync pages QBO /query via in-query STARTPOSITION/MAXRESULTS until a short page; without it the mirror truncates at 1000 rows/entity
handfixes.why: resourceSupportsPagination() is false for every QBO entity, so the generic sync loop breaks after page 1 - capping each entity at one 1000-row page (QBO's MAXRESULTS hard cap). On a real book this silently truncated catastrophically: 1000 of 17,940 invoices, 1000 of 16,863 payments, making ar-aging/ap-aging/dso/balances wrong (not just partial), and QBO returns rows oldest-first so recent invoices were missing entirely. Fix carries the next STARTPOSITION in the loop's `cursor`, sets hasMore from full-page detection (len(items) >= qboMaxResults), and exempts /query from the generic !resourceSupportsPagination and short-page breaks. Verified live against production Servosity: all 8 resources sync to their exact live count(*) (44,211 records, invoices 17,940/17,940).
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Same press change as qbo-query-injection: a QBO sync-hint that emits STARTPOSITION/MAXRESULTS paging by construction.

handfixes.summary: page-item extraction unwraps QBO's two-level `{"QueryResponse":{"<Entity>":[...]}}` envelope
handfixes.why: QBO /query nests the result array one level deeper than the generic extractor handles: `QueryResponse` (object) then the PascalCase entity key (array). Without `QueryResponse` in dataEnvelopeKeys, extractPageItems cannot reach the entity array, returns 0 items, and sync falls through to single-object upsert of the whole envelope. Adding it lets the existing two-level unwrap + single-array-sibling logic pull the entity array correctly. Paired with qbo-query-injection; both are required for sync to store any rows.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Same press change as qbo-query-injection: a QBO response-envelope hint so the generated extractor unwraps QueryResponse.<Entity> by construction.

handfixes.summary: defaultDBPath honors QUICKBOOKS_DB_PATH so sandbox and production keep separate local mirrors
handfixes.why: The local SQLite mirror path was hardcoded to ~/.local/share/quickbooks-cli/data.db with no per-tenant separation. QBO entity IDs are per-company and collide across companies (sandbox Id "58" vs production Id "58"), so running both sandbox and production against one data.db corrupts aggregates after a mode switch. The Keychain wrapper sets QUICKBOOKS_DB_PATH per mode (.../sandbox/data.db vs .../production/data.db); this env override makes every command (sync, doctor, the aggregates) honor it.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Press change: a generic <CLI>_DB_PATH env override on defaultDBPath would let any multi-tenant connector isolate mirrors without a hand-edit.

handfixes.summary: aging-delta creates its aging_snapshots table on demand; no store migration defines it
handfixes.why: aging_delta.go (a novel hand-authored command) reads and writes an aging_snapshots table, but nothing in the store migrations creates it. So every aging-delta run errored `no such table: aging_snapshots` on the read path (loadLatestAgingSnapshot treats only sql.ErrNoRows as a clean first-run; a missing table is a hard error) - the command was broken from first use. Fix adds ensureAgingSnapshotsTable (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) called before the read, so a fresh or freshly reprinted DB self-heals. aging_delta.go is a novel file kept whole by regen-merge, so this survives a reprint; this entry is the cheap backstop the reprint-survival doc recommends for novel commands.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Press change: novel commands that own a private table should declare it in the store migration set so it is created by construction.

asserts.contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS aging_snapshots
